ground.schema:
Node properties:
User {betweenness: FLOAT, location: STRING, followers: INTEGER, following: INTEGER, profile_image_url: STRING, screen_name: STRING, name: STRING, url: STRING, statuses: INTEGER}
Me {profile_image_url: STRING, betweenness: FLOAT, following: INTEGER, url: STRING, location: STRING, followers: INTEGER, screen_name: STRING, name: STRING}
Tweet {created_at: DATE_TIME, id: INTEGER, id_str: STRING, text: STRING, favorites: INTEGER, import_method: STRING}
Hashtag {name: STRING}
Link {url: STRING}
Source {name: STRING}
Relationship properties:
SIMILAR_TO {score: FLOAT}
The relationships:
(:User)-[:FOLLOWS]->(:User)
(:User)-[:FOLLOWS]->(:Me)
(:User)-[:POSTS]->(:Tweet)
(:User)-[:INTERACTS_WITH]->(:User)
(:User)-[:SIMILAR_TO]->(:User)
(:User)-[:SIMILAR_TO]->(:Me)
(:Me)-[:FOLLOWS]->(:User)
(:Me)-[:POSTS]->(:Tweet)
(:Me)-[:INTERACTS_WITH]->(:User)
(:Me)-[:RT_MENTIONS]->(:User)
(:Me)-[:AMPLIFIES]->(:User)
(:Tweet)-[:MENTIONS]->(:User)
(:Tweet)-[:MENTIONS]->(:Me)
(:Tweet)-[:USING]->(:Source)
(:Tweet)-[:TAGS]->(:Hashtag)
(:Tweet)-[:CONTAINS]->(:Link)
(:Tweet)-[:RETWEETS]->(:Tweet)
(:Tweet)-[:REPLY_TO]->(:Tweet)
ground.cypher:
MATCH
    p1 = (a:User {name: 'TechEnthusiast'})-[r1:FOLLOWS]->(b:User),
    p2 = (a)-[r2:FOLLOWS]->(c:Me {screen_name: 'TechGuru'}),
    p3 = (b)-[r3:POSTS]->(d:Tweet)-[r4:TAGS]->(e:Hashtag {name: '#Tech'}),
    p4 = (d)-[r5:CONTAINS]->(f:Link {url: 'https://example.com/tech'})-[r6:USING]->(g:Source {name: 'TechBlog'})
RETURN p1, p2, p3, p4, p5:d, p6:g
ground.question: How do the followers of TechEnthusiast influence the engagement patterns of their tweets about tech-related topics, particularly those that link to TechBlog’s content?

cypher:
MATCH (u:User {screen_name: 'TechEnthusiast'})-[:POSTS]->(t:Tweet)-[:CONTAINS]->(l:Link {url: 'techblog.com'})
WITH u, t
MATCH (u)-[:SIMILAR_TO]->(follower:User)
WITH follower, t
RETURN follower.screen_name AS follower, t.text AS tweet, t.favorites AS favorites
